free_text: Cross-section measurements to low-lying excited
final states in the 24Mg(alpha,p)27Al*(gamma) reaction
as an energy source for x-ray bursts
Sunkyunkwan University, Suwon
The experiment was performed using
5 MeV Sta. ANA accelerator at the Nuclear Science
Laboratory at the University of Notre Dame.
Compiled by S.H.
Thin magnesium target with a
thickness of 34.9 mu-g/cm2 produced by evaporating
enriched 24Mg magnesium metal onto a 0.2 mm thick
tantalum backing.
Secondary rays were detected using an array
of 13  2" x 2"  LaBr3 detectors. The detectors were
placed approximately 12" from the center of the target
position from 0 to 120 deg.
Secondary  gamma ray yields  were extracted from the
LaBr3 energy spectra for gamma rays at Eg = 0.844 and
1.014 MeV, which correspond to decays from the first
two excited states of 27Al directly to the ground
state for of 27Al. The normalized differential
thick-target yields were integrated over the angular
range using a Legendre polynomial fit.
Origin of uncertainties not described
Data taken from fig.7 of the reference.

Reaction rate was calculated by integration of the
cubic spline fit of the angle-integrated yields
determined in C2697002.
